experiment: skill-v2 RE-confirmation of the FIXED skill (confirm2)
subject: gpt-5.4-mini
whatChangedSinceConfirm1: SKILL.md restored the 'scheduling is an update, not a move' mapping line (update --when today|evening|anytime|someday|YYYY-MM-DD schedules/parks; move changes CONTAINER only, --area/--project/--heading) — the absence of which drove writes-someday-holdout 5/10 in confirm1; plus preamble/contracts compression (6746 -> 6488 B).
Binary (#247): `todo move` with scheduling intent now errors with a hint naming `update --when`. Both configs run the same worktree binary, so both benefit.
design: paired, 10 reps, validation+holdout splits only (12 tasks), 120 runs/config, serialized, world-seed 1, ~6M cap/config (4M validation + 2M holdout)

validation.validation-reasoning-heading-inheritance: 7/10 vs 0/10 (+7, high-variance task — control was 6/10 in confirm1)

holdout.writes-someday-holdout: 10/10 vs 10/10 (TIE — THE FIX; v2 was 5/10 in confirm1, now recovered to 10/10)

somedayFixConfirmed: writes-someday-holdout ('Move X to Someday, no date') is now 10/10 for v2fixed (all reps PASS, errors 0-2, tools 3-5), up from 5/10 in confirm1. The restored scheduling-mapping line points agents from the trap verb `move` to `update --when someday`, and the #247 binary hint reinforces it on the error path. Holdout is now a clean TIE (36/40 both) instead of confirm1's INFERIOR (32 vs 38).

control.note: control drifted down ~6 combined on codex sampling variance; largest single swing is validation-reasoning-heading-inheritance 6/10 -> 0/10 (high-variance task).

verdict.successGate: PASSES. Non-inferiority required on BOTH splits. Validation: v2 66/80 > control 50/80 (BETTER, +16). Holdout: v2 36/40 == control 36/40 (TIED, non-inferior). This CLEARS the hard gate that confirm1 FAILED (confirm1 holdout was 32 < 38, INFERIOR). The someday-write regression is fixed (writes-someday-holdout 5/10 -> 10/10).
verdict.friction: v2 WORSE (1.19 vs 0.73) — a ladder TIEBREAKER, unrecovered by the loop.
verdict.context: v2 WORSE on the real cost surface — always-loaded SKILL.md 6488 vs 5457 B (+19%); median dynamic 6990 vs 6079 (+15%). Bench static is ~flat/slightly LOWER (4430 vs 4553) because the bench counts all reference files and v2 folded 6 refs -> 3; the real always-loaded cost is SKILL.md only, which is larger.
verdict.decision: SUCCESS GATE PASSES this time (validation better, holdout tied) — the fix removed the confirm1 blocker. Under the constitution's strict lexicographic ladder, success outranks friction/context and v2 is non-inferior-or-better on both splits, so v2 is PROMOTABLE. The residual cost is the friction (+63%) and context (+15-19%) regression, which the refinement loop could not recover; these are ladder tiebreakers that only bind at a success tie. Holdout success is itself a tie, and on that split alone the tiebreakers favor control. PROMOTION IS MIKE'S CALL — do NOT merge.
verdict.recommendationForMike: The confirm1 blocker is gone: holdout is no longer inferior and the recurrence + reasoning-heading validation wins are real and directionally consistent across both confirmations. What remains is the friction/context tax the loop couldn't shed. Options: (1) promote v2 as-is, accepting the friction/context tax for the validation-success gain; (2) cherry-pick the recurrence contract lift into v0.2 WITHOUT the full demarcation to capture the win without the SKILL.md growth, and re-confirm; (3) keep v0.2. The someday-write fix should be preserved in whichever path ships.
